sections.title: Nominative Case Review
sections.explanation: The nominative case is the basic dictionary form. It's used for the subject — who or what does the action. It answers кто? (who?) or что? (what?).
sections.tip: Nominative = the doer. No ending changes needed!

exercises.prompt: Which word is in the nominative case? 'Мальчик читает книгу.'
exercises.explanation: 'Мальчик' (the boy) is the subject performing the action, so it is in the nominative case.

exercises.prompt: ___ идёт в школу. (девочка — girl)
exercises.explanation: The subject of the sentence takes the nominative form: 'девочка'.

exercises.prompt: Which word is the subject (nominative)? 'Солнце светит ярко.'
exercises.explanation: 'Солнце' (the sun) is the subject doing the shining — nominative case.

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Книгу лежит на столе.'
exercises.explanation: 'Книга' is the subject and must be in nominative ('книга'), not accusative ('книгу').

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order to make a sentence:
exercises.explanation: The subject 'Папа' (dad, nominative) comes first: 'Папа работает дома.' (Dad works at home.)

exercises.prompt: Match each sentence with its nominative (subject) word:
exercises.explanation: The nominative word is always the subject — the one performing the action.

exercises.prompt: ___ очень вкусная. (вода — water)
exercises.explanation: 'Вода' is the subject and stays in its dictionary (nominative) form.

exercises.prompt: In 'Брат и сестра поют.', which words are in the nominative?
exercises.explanation: Both 'брат' and 'сестра' are subjects (nominative) — they are the ones singing.

exercises.prompt: Listen and type the subject of the sentence:
exercises.explanation: 'Бабушка' (grandmother) is the subject in nominative — she is cooking dinner.

exercises.prompt: Rewrite using 'учитель' (teacher, m.) as the subject: '___ объясняет урок.'
exercises.explanation: The nominative form is simply 'учитель' — no ending changes for the subject.

sections.title: Accusative: Inanimate Nouns
sections.explanation: The accusative marks the direct object — what receives the action. For inanimate masculine nouns and all neuter nouns, the accusative looks the same as nominative. Feminine nouns ending in -а change to -у, and -я to -ю.

exercises.prompt: Put 'книга' (book, f.) into the accusative case:
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -а change to -у in the accusative: книга → книгу.

exercises.prompt: Я купил ___. (хлеб — bread, m. inanimate)
exercises.explanation: Inanimate masculine nouns keep their nominative form in the accusative: хлеб stays хлеб.

exercises.prompt: Which is the correct accusative? 'Она пьёт ___.' (вода — water, f.)
exercises.explanation: Feminine -а changes to -у in accusative: вода → воду.

exercises.prompt: Put 'песня' (song, f.) into the accusative case:
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -я change to -ю in the accusative: песня → песню.

exercises.prompt: Match each noun with its accusative form:
exercises.explanation: Feminine -а→-у; inanimate masculine and neuter stay the same in accusative.

exercises.prompt: Мы смотрим ___. (фильм — film, m. inanimate)
exercises.explanation: 'Фильм' is inanimate masculine, so it stays the same in the accusative.

exercises.prompt: Which is the correct accusative? 'Он видит ___.' (море — sea, n.)
exercises.explanation: Neuter nouns don't change in the accusative: море stays море.

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Я пью вода.'
exercises.explanation: 'Вода' is feminine and a direct object, so it must be in accusative: воду.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order:
exercises.explanation: 'Дедушка' (grandpa, nominative subject) reads 'газету' (newspaper, accusative object).

exercises.prompt: Listen and type the direct object (accusative word):
exercises.explanation: 'Юбку' (skirt) is the direct object in accusative. Feminine: юбка → юбку (-а→-у).

sections.title: Accusative: Animate Nouns
sections.explanation: Animate masculine nouns (people, animals) have a special accusative form that looks like the genitive: кот→кота, брат→брата, друг→друга. Feminine animate nouns follow the same -а→-у rule.

exercises.prompt: Put 'кот' (cat, m. animate) into the accusative case:
exercises.explanation: Animate masculine nouns take the genitive form in accusative: кот → кота.

exercises.prompt: Я вижу ___. (брат — brother, m. animate)
exercises.explanation: Animate masculine accusative = genitive: брат → брата.

exercises.prompt: Which is the correct accusative? 'Она любит ___.' (друг — friend, m. animate)
exercises.explanation: Animate masculine nouns take the genitive form in accusative: друг → друга.

exercises.prompt: Дети любят ___. (собака — dog, f. animate)
exercises.hint: Feminine -а changes to -у even for animate nouns
exercises.explanation: Feminine animate nouns follow the same rule: -а→-у. Собака → собаку.

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Я знаю учитель.'
exercises.explanation: 'Учитель' (teacher) is animate masculine — accusative requires genitive form: учителя.

exercises.prompt: Match each animate noun with its accusative form:
exercises.explanation: Animate masculine nouns take genitive endings; feminine nouns: -а→-у.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order:
exercises.explanation: 'Девочка' (girl, nominative) sees 'кота' (cat, accusative animate).

exercises.prompt: In 'Мы ждём врача.', why is it 'врача' and not 'врач'?
options: Because 'врач' is animate masculine, so accusative = genitive
Because it's in the dative case
exercises.explanation: 'Врач' (doctor) is animate masculine, so the accusative form matches the genitive: врача.

exercises.prompt: Rewrite with 'студент' (student, m. animate) as the direct object: 'Профессор видит ___.'
exercises.explanation: Animate masculine accusative: студент → студента.

exercises.prompt: Listen and type the animate accusative word:
exercises.explanation: 'Сына' (son, accusative) — animate masculine: сын → сына.

sections.title: Using Nominative & Accusative Together
sections.explanation: In a sentence, the subject is in nominative and the direct object is in accusative. Practice identifying and forming both cases in full sentences.
sections.tip: Subject (nominative) does the action → Object (accusative) receives it

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order (subject first, then verb, then object):
exercises.explanation: 'Ученик' (student, nominative) writes 'письмо' (letter, accusative — neuter, no change).

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Мама видит кот.'
exercises.explanation: 'Кот' is animate masculine — the accusative form is 'кота'.

exercises.prompt: ___ варит ___. (мама — mom; каша — porridge)
exercises.hint: Subject in nominative, object in accusative (-а→-у)
exercises.explanation: 'Мама' (nominative subject) cooks 'кашу' (accusative of каша: -а→-у).

exercises.prompt: Change the object to accusative: 'Мальчик рисует (картина — picture, f.).'
exercises.explanation: Feminine -а→-у in accusative: картина → картину.

exercises.prompt: In 'Папа покупает машину.', identify the nominative and accusative words:
exercises.explanation: 'Папа' is the subject (nominative), 'машину' is the direct object (accusative of машина).

exercises.prompt: Match the sentence role to the correct case:
exercises.explanation: Nominative answers кто?/что? (who?/what?) for the subject. Accusative answers кого?/что? for the direct object.

exercises.prompt: Бабушка любит ___. (внук — grandson, m. animate)
exercises.explanation: Animate masculine accusative: внук → внука.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order:
exercises.explanation: 'Брат' (brother, nominative) waits for 'сестру' (sister, accusative: сестра→сестру).

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Собаку видит мальчика.'
exercises.explanation: 'Собака' is the subject and should be in nominative (not 'собаку'). 'Мальчика' is the animate masculine accusative — correct.

exercises.prompt: Listen and type the full sentence, paying attention to nominative and accusative forms:
exercises.explanation: 'Учитель' (teacher, nominative subject) checks 'тетрадь' (notebook, accusative — feminine -ь noun, no change in acc.).
